audits.scope: Sui Move bytecode verifier, core consensus logic
audits.finding: Critical bug in move-binary-format crate relating to bytecode control flow verification discovered pre-mainnet; silently fixed March 30, 2023. Zellic report titled 'The Billion Dollar Bug'
audits.impact: Bytecode verifier vulnerability posed potential billions in risk

audits.scope: Sui ecosystem and Move language security assessments
audits.finding: Partnership announced December 2023; conducting multiple audits of Sui projects and core modules

exploit_history.name: Cetus Protocol Exploit (Sui Ecosystem)
exploit_history.root_cause: Arithmetic overflow in third-party math library (integer-mate) used for liquidity calculations; faulty overflow check in u256 checked_shlw function allowed attacker to pass validation but trigger overflow during computation
exploit_history.vector: Spoof token attack: attacker created spoofed tokens mimicking legitimate assets (e.g., BULLA) and extracted ~$223M via liquidity pool exploitation
exploit_history.fund_recovery: Sui validators froze ~$162M on-chain; ~$61M bridged to Ethereum before freezing. Protocol restarted with $30M Sui Foundation loan; recovered ~85% of initial liquidity value
exploit_history.impact_on_l1: Ecosystem exploit, NOT L1 vulnerability; Sui consensus and core protocol functioned correctly during attack
exploit_history.notes: Largest DeFi exploit on Sui to date; demonstrates need for stronger auditing of ecosystem dependencies

exploit_history.name: Aftermath Finance Exploit (Sui Ecosystem)
exploit_history.notes: Smaller scope than Cetus; specific perpetual contracts module vulnerability

exploit_history.name: Scallop Protocol Exploit (Sui Ecosystem)
exploit_history.impact_on_l1: Ecosystem exploit in deprecated module, NOT L1 vulnerability
exploit_history.notes: Targeted deprecated/side module rather than core infrastructure

exploit_history.name: Volo Protocol Issue (Sui Ecosystem)
exploit_history.notes: Admin key management issue rather than protocol flaw

code_health.commit_cadence: High frequency; main branch receives continuous updates
code_health.contributors: Large active team; multiple branches (main, devnet, testnet, mainnet)
code_health.code_review_process: GitHub PR-based; appears to have standard review workflow
code_health.formal_verification: Move bytecode verifier underwent formal scrutiny; no evidence of formal verification for consensus protocol
code_health.testing_framework: Standard Rust testing; crates structure suggests modular approach
code_health.decentralization: 100 active validators with on-chain governance; Sui Foundation initially stakes across entire set; validator set voting power >2/3 required for consensus (Byzantine Fault Tolerance)
code_health.upgrade_mechanism: On-chain governance via SUI token holder voting; validators propose and approve protocol upgrades through consensus-based voting
code_health.note: Critical pre-mainnet bug (Move bytecode) was fixed silently; this highlights importance of pre-launch audits

dependencies.name: integer-mate
dependencies.risk: Cetus exploit traced to faulty overflow check in this library; not core L1 dependency but impacts ecosystem security
dependencies.note: External dependency risk in Move ecosystem

rationale.assessment: Sui L1 demonstrates solid security posture for core protocol. Pre-mainnet Zellic audit of Move bytecode verifier caught critical bug, showing strong pre-launch security discipline. Active partnerships with OtterSec and Zellic provide ongoing ecosystem security. No known L1-level exploits or consensus failures; all major exploits (Cetus $223M, Aftermath $1.1M, Scallop $450K) were ecosystem contract bugs, not core protocol vulnerabilities. Validator set of ~100 with >2/3 BFT threshold provides reasonable decentralization. On-chain governance allows community-driven upgrades. Primary security risk lies in Move ecosystem dependency quality (e.g., integer-mate library), not L1 consensus or bytecode verifier.
rationale.gaps: No public evidence of formal verification for Mysticeti consensus protocol. Validator set composition and decentralization metrics could be stronger (100 validators is moderate; some chains target 150+). Limited public detail on network-level security testing or fuzzing campaigns.
rationale.confidence: High for L1 security posture; High for ecosystem risk assessment given Cetus incident data; Medium for detailed validator decentralization metrics

data_quality.sources: Free public sources; official Sui documentation, GitHub, OtterSec/Zellic public statements, Immunefi, news reports
data_quality.completeness: Comprehensive for major audits and exploits; limited detail on all historical patches/vulnerabilities
data_quality.recency: Data through April 2025 (Scallop exploit) and current as of April 2026; Cetus exploit details from May 2025
data_quality.verification: Cross-referenced across multiple sources; Cetus incident well-documented by Cyfrin, Elliptic, Halborn
